subject: Ecology
title: Smoke-Stimulated Germination
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Smoke-Stimulated Germination.

hook.text: Thousands of plant species won't germinate unless their seeds detect chemicals in smoke.

buildup.text: Karrikinolide, a compound in smoke, triggers germination in species worldwide.

discovery.text: South Africa's fynbos uses smoke signals—after fire, wildflowers carpet the ash within weeks.

twist.text: Nurseries now use liquid smoke to germinate fire-dependent seeds for restoration projects.

climax.text: Smoke-responsive seeds can lie dormant for decades, waiting for the right chemical cue.

punchline.text: These seeds don't need water first. They need smoke.

quiz.question: What chemical in smoke triggers seed germination?
